mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present values of S&P 500 equities and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of co-occurring growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates sustained manufacturing output that supports earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of observations during late-cycle expansions, or Rank IC turns negative when GDPC1 growth decelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP reflect stronger aggregate economic activity that directly boosts corporate profit expectations, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.2 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2%, or the signal fails to predict returns when FEDFUNDS is already elevated.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates negative 20-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates weakening manufacturing demand that immediately pressures equity prices, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of months where INDPRO declines during tightening cycles, or the signal shows no predictive power when FEDFUNDS is stable.
